---
title: "Specifications"
---

> **What this page is:** The normative contracts for cositos — protocol, serialization,
> embed, lifecycle, and lifecycle-shell — woven from `openspec/specs/`.

> **Reading on GitHub?** This page is woven from spec files in `openspec/specs/` via
> Quarto's `{{< include >}}`. The raw spec files render directly on GitHub:
> `openspec/specs/{protocol,serialization,embed,lifecycle,lifecycle-shell}/spec.md`.

These are the **living specifications** for cositos — the source-of-truth contracts that
the test suite and any cross-language port certify against. They are maintained under
`openspec/specs/` and woven into this page verbatim, so the docs can never drift from the
spec: this page *is* the spec.

Each specification lists **requirements** (normative statements) and **scenarios**
(Given/When/Then examples that pin the behaviour). The [API reference](index.qmd) links
back here from each concern.

## protocol {#protocol}

The pure message-shaping and parsing contract: how state becomes `comm_open` / `update` /
`custom` payloads, how inbound messages are parsed, and how binary buffers split and
merge losslessly.

{{< include ../../openspec/specs/protocol/spec.md >}}

## serialization {#serialization}

How a widget (or a composed graph of widgets) round-trips through JSON — including
base64-encoded binary buffers and the document envelope's state-format version.

{{< include ../../openspec/specs/serialization/spec.md >}}

## embed {#embed}

How a serialized document renders to self-contained HTML via the CDN html-manager, and
how embedded JSON is script-escaped so widget state can't break out of the page.

{{< include ../../openspec/specs/embed/spec.md >}}

## lifecycle {#lifecycle}

The pure widget lifecycle reducer: a deterministic state machine that encodes the
open → send → close lifecycle as a pure function. Every cositos backend feeds events
(Open, SendState, SendCustom, Inbound, Close, CommIdAssigned) through `reduce()` and
executes the returned effects. See the [API reference](index.qmd#lifecycle) for the
event and effect types.

{{< include ../../openspec/specs/lifecycle/spec.md >}}

## lifecycle-shell {#lifecycle-shell}

The thin imperative shell that calls the lifecycle reducer and executes the returned
effects — bridging between the pure reducer and the real transport/kernel APIs. See the
[API reference](index.qmd#lifecycle) for the shell API and capability types.

{{< include ../../openspec/specs/lifecycle-shell/spec.md >}}
